title: 1.1: Reports WebSocket status messages (e.g., STARTING, WORKING, SUCCESS) as runtime signals indicating OCR progress
1.2: Identifies log entries indicating OCR execution (e.g., "Calling OCRmyPDF with args") as a runtime signal
1.3: Reports that the consume_file task is synchronous and blocks the worker during OCR processing
1.4: Identifies that with OCR_MODE skip the OCR pipeline is still invoked with skip_text=True parameter
1.5: Reports that with OCR_MODE skip_noarchive the system skips calling ocrmypdf entirely for text-containing documents
1.6: Reports how to tell the difference after processing finishes (e.g., has_archive_version is true with skip mode, false with skip_noarchive)
1.7: Reports comparison of API metadata fields (e.g., original_mime_type, has_archive_version, archive_metadata) that indicate OCR-generated versus existing text
1.8: Reports that documents with poor or incomplete OCR results are still saved and count as fully processed
1.9: Identifies that incomplete OCR is reflected in the content field (e.g., empty or shorter content)
2.1: Claims that OCR uses direct subprocess calls to invoke tesseract
